name: nix-patterns
description: NixOS module patterns. Use when creating or editing NixOS/home-manager modules, adding packages, or configuring programs.
capabilities: Shows directory structure for NixOS modules
Explains module imports and relative paths
author: EdgarPost
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing static NixOS configuration patterns in markdown. No executable code, network calls, or file operations. All 24 static findings are false positives: the scanner misinterprets Nix language syntax (with pkgs, lib.optionals, builtins.toJSON) as shell commands, relative import paths as path traversal, and JSON metadata fields containing 'hash' substrings as cryptographic operations.
valueStatement: NixOS module configuration requires understanding specific syntax and patterns. This skill provides ready-to-use examples for home-manager modules, package installation, and system configuration.
limitations: Does not include advanced Nix features like overlays
Limited to home-manager and NixOS configurations

useCases.title: Standardize Configurations
useCases.description: Use proven patterns to maintain consistent NixOS systems across environments.

useCases.title: Deploy User Environments
useCases.description: Configure home environments with home-manager using tested module patterns.

useCases.title: Share Configurations
useCases.description: Create modular NixOS configs that teams can share and reuse across machines.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Module
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a home-manager module that installs Firefox and configures settings.

promptTemplates.title: Conditional Packages
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pattern for installing packages only on x86_64-linux systems.

promptTemplates.title: Service Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Provide a NixOS module pattern for enabling and configuring SSH daemon.

promptTemplates.title: Modular Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a configuration that separates development tools from user preferences.

bestPractices: Use descriptive module names that indicate purpose
Test configurations in a VM before deployment
antiPatterns: Avoid putting all configurations in a single large file
Do not hardcode system-specific paths or usernames
Never skip testing after major NixOS updates

faq.question: Is this compatible with all NixOS versions?
faq.answer: Uses standard syntax that works with recent stable versions.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns with flakes?
faq.answer: Module patterns work with both traditional and flake-based configurations.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing configs?
faq.answer: Import modules in your home/default.nix or configuration.nix file.

faq.question: Are these patterns safe for production?
faq.answer: Standard configuration patterns used in production deployments.

faq.question: What if my package is not in nixpkgs?
faq.answer: Custom packages or overlays are not covered in this skill.

faq.question: Can I share configs between machines?
faq.answer: Yes, modular configurations make sharing across systems easy.
